parameters.content:
### On New Email (IMAP)

Watches the IMAP inbox and emits new emails as items (subject, body, attachments).

Next: Prepare Approval Packet

🔑 IMAP credentials

### Prepare Approval Packet

Shapes the item: writes `requestId`, `summary`, `requestedBy`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Request Approval via Telegram

### Request Approval via Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Next: Hold for Decision

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Hold for Decision

Pauses the execution until the resume webhook is called — this is the approval/HITL gate.

Next: Approved?

### Approved?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.body?.decision || $json.decision }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Apply Approved Change (Airtable) · ②→ Record Decline + Notify Requester

### Apply Approved Change (Airtable)

Upserts into Airtable's 'Invoices' table, matched on `invoiceId` — safe to re-run without creating duplicates.

Next: Join Decision Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Record Decline + Notify Requester

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Next: Join Decision Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Join Decision Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

# Track receipts with Airtable and Telegram on autopilot

**Pattern:** Expense Tracking · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Email

## What it does
Track receipts with Airtable and Telegram on autopilot. It reacts to incoming email.

## Why it exists
Manual expenses is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
On New Email (IMAP) → Prepare Approval Packet → Request Approval via Telegram → Hold for Decision → Approved? → Apply Approved Change (Airtable) → Join Decision Paths → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to expense tracking without manual effort
- Anyone automating expenses in finance
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Standardizes the process every time
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; IMAP credentials; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The workflow pauses on a Wait node configured to resume via webhook — n8n's actual human-in-the-loop primitive, not a polling loop. The resume URL is embedded directly in the approval message so approving is one click, and the paused execution burns no compute for however long the decision takes.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a timeout to the Wait node so unanswered approvals auto-decline after N hours.
2. Change the approval channel and confirm the resume link still works.
3. Add a third outcome (e.g. 'needs changes') alongside approve/decline.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🙋 Human in the loop

The workflow pauses at the Wait node until a human posts a decision to the resume URL (included in the approval message). Approve → the action executes; anything else → recorded as declined. No timeout is set by default — add one under the Wait node's options if approvals go stale.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your expenses process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
